components.name: HeroPrimary
components.purpose: Page-opening statement. Establishes context and intent above the fold.
components.notes: Validator variant always includes trust_signal. Mover variant suppresses subhead and cta_secondary — one message, one action. with-social-proof variant places LogoStrip immediately below CTACluster.

components.name: HeroStatement
components.purpose: Text-heavy opening for pages where the argument is the product. No media distraction.
components.notes: Best for thought leadership, product philosophy, and narrative-led pages. Explorer variant uses longer body copy and suppresses primary CTA pressure. Validator variant uses with-eyebrow to anchor credibility before headline lands.

components.name: FeatureGrid
components.purpose: Structured articulation of product capabilities. Three-column icon + copy layout.
components.notes: Validator benefits from specific, outcome-oriented descriptions — not feature names, benefit statements. Explorer variant uses 2-col-heavy with longer copy per feature. 3-col-icon is the default; use for 6 or 9 features. list-style for 4–5 features with uneven copy length.

components.name: SocialProofBar
components.purpose: Establishes credibility through recognizable brand logos or a featured quote.
components.notes: Mover variant is logos-only, no headline — silent credibility. Validator variant leads with quote, logos below. logos-only is the highest-frequency placement; drop directly below HeroPrimary on awareness pages.

components.name: StatBlock
components.purpose: High-impact metric clusters that establish scale, outcome, or momentum.
components.notes: Always pair stats with source attribution in Validator contexts — unsourced stats reduce trust. Mover variant suppresses sources and headline; pure metric impact. 3-stat is the preferred default. 4-stat requires strong visual balance.

components.name: PricingCard
components.purpose: Conversion-optimized pricing display. One plan per card.
components.notes: highlighted variant for recommended plan — use sparingly (one per pricing section). Validator variant includes description and longer feature list. Mover variant strips description; price and CTA only. with-comparison for enterprise tiers where feature differentiation drives upgrade.

components.name: TestimonialSingle
components.purpose: Single featured testimonial with attribution. Primary trust signal for Validator archetype.
components.notes: Always include role and company for Validator credibility — anonymous quotes carry no weight. with-photo increases engagement and perceived authenticity. with-company-logo for enterprise social proof. Place near a CTA — testimonials convert when positioned at a decision moment.

components.name: CTABanner
components.purpose: Full-width conversion prompt. Terminal action for a section or page.
components.notes: Mover variant: strong headline, single CTA, no subhead. Validator variant: include subhead and trust_signal (e.g., 'No credit card required'). minimal for high-frequency use — above footer, between sections. split for section-end with directional copy on left, CTA on right.

components.name: ContentSection
components.purpose: Long-form body content for Validator-optimized pages. Structured argument.
components.notes: Designed for depth and argument. Explorer variant uses with-sidebar with related links. Validator benefits from structured subheadings within body. with-callout for key proof points that deserve visual separation. Don't use on Mover pages.

components.name: NavigationHeader
components.purpose: Global navigation. Sets wayfinding context and primary conversion action for the session.
components.notes: minimal variant for conversion-focused pages — strip nav_links, keep logo and single CTA only. transparent for hero sections with media background. full for exploration and top-funnel pages. The persistent CTA in nav is the most converted element on most SaaS pages — never suppress it.

components.name: FooterMinimal
components.purpose: Page close. Brand mark, minimal navigation, legal, and optional recovery CTA.
components.notes: with-cta variant recaptures departing users on key conversion pages — use on pricing and signup flows. minimal strips nav_columns and social_links; logo + legal only. standard is the default. Keep legal_text concise.
